definition.usage_explanation_long: Scans paginated chat history for recurring temporal, behavioural, emotional, and relational patterns and records each as a sensitivity-classified observation.
definition.usage_explanation_short: detect recurring behavioural patterns in chat data
definition.system_prompt:
# Pattern Observer

Scan chat history for recurring patterns and record each as a new observation:
- Temporal patterns (time-based behaviours)
- Behavioural patterns (consistent actions)
- Emotional patterns (mood trends)
- Relational patterns (interaction styles)

Paginate the chat history so the dataset is comprehensive (e.g. 30 days,
200 messages per page, advancing the offset until a short page returns), then
analyse ALL collected messages before recording observations.

When recording, classify sensitivity: `public` (default, safe for any model),
`nsfw` (intimate patterns, local models only), or `secret` (highly private,
local models only).

agent_tests.name: profile/pattern_observer::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
Please analyze the following paginated team chat history and identify all recurring temporal, behavioural, emotional, and relational patterns across participants. Record each pattern as a sensitivity-classified observation (Low / Medium / High sensitivity).

--- PAGE 1 (Jan 2–15, 2024) ---
[09:03] Sarah K.: Good morning team! Ready to tackle the sprint goals today?
[09:05] Mike T.: Morning. Sure.
[09:06] Elena R.: Let's do it! 💪
[09:15] Sarah K.: @Mike T. can you share the latest API docs?
[09:45] Mike T.: https://docs.internal/api-v3
[10:02] Elena R.: These docs are outdated, we need updated ones
[10:05] Mike T.: That's what we have. Deal with it.
[10:06] Sarah K.: Mike, let's be constructive please. I'll check with the backend team.
[14:30] Elena R.: anyone else drowning in tickets? 😫
[14:32] Sarah K.: Hang in there, I'll reprioritize the board after standup tomorrow
[17:55] Mike T.: Logging off. Late dinner.
[22:10] Elena R.: Finally finished the migration… exhausted

--- PAGE 2 (Jan 16–31, 2024) ---
[09:02] Sarah K.: Morning everyone! Big week ahead 🌟
[09:04] Mike T.: Morning.
[09:07] Elena R.: Already on my second coffee lol ☕☕
[09:20] Sarah K.: @Mike T. the staging env is down again
[09:50] Mike T.: Looking into it.
[10:30] Mike T.: Fixed. Was a config issue.
[10:31] Elena R.: Thanks Mike!
[10:32] Mike T.: 👍
[14:45] Elena R.: this codebase is a nightmare, who wrote the auth module??
[14:47] Sarah K.: Let's focus on solutions, not blame. Want to pair on it?
[14:48] Elena R.: sorry, yeah that would help
[18:02] Mike T.: Done for the day.
[23:45] Elena R.: pushed the hotfix, going to sleep

--- PAGE 3 (Feb 1–14, 2024) ---
[09:01] Sarah K.: Happy Friday team! Almost through another sprint!
[09:03] Mike T.: Morning.
[09:08] Elena R.: Morning!! Big news - I got promoted!! 🎉🎉🎉
[09:09] Sarah K.: OH MY GOD CONGRATS ELENA!!! 🥳🥳
[09:10] Mike T.: Congrats.
[09:15] Sarah K.: We should celebrate! Team lunch?
[09:16] Mike T.: I have a dentist appointment.
[09:17] Elena R.: haha classic Mike 😂
[14:30] Elena R.: @Sarah K. can we talk about workload? I've been doing overtime every day this month
[14:32] Sarah K.: Absolutely, let's book a 1:1. I've been worried about your hours.
[14:33] Elena R.: thanks, I appreciate it
[18:00] Mike T.: Heading out.
[01:20] Elena R.: why do I do this to myself… still working

Please scan all three pages and extract every recurring pattern you find.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Scans paginated chat history for recurring temporal, behavioural, emotional, and relational patterns and records each as a sensitivity-classified observation.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: pattern-observer-records-via-profile-manager
capability_tests.description: Records observations via profile-manager; never write/edit.
